observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjusting entries to record the valid reconciling items. Likewise, trace remaining errors to substantiate unreconciled amounts and make necessary adjustments in the books, if warranted.

We recommended that the Municipality expedite in facilitation of the request for write-off/derecognition of its Other Receivables account. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant shall provide complete details such as the exact dates when the receivables were granted and the updated statuses of the employees, whether resigned/transferred/dead, in the monitoring report of the Other Receivables account.

In view of the foregoing, we recommend that Management undertake the following:
To make necessary adjustments for the above-discussed overstatement of Construction in Progress and Accounts Payable accounts; and
Record/recognize infrastructure projects on a billing/accomplishment basis of the contractors in compliance with Sections 50 and 52(d), Chapter 3, Volume I of the New Government Accounting System Manual for LGUs.
Lastly, the Audit Team would like to commend the Municipality’s efforts to comply with DBM, DOF, and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1, dated November 4, 2020, which resulted in no expenses charged that were not related to DF.

We commended Management on swift action to utilize its Quick Response Fund of LDRRMF for relief to affected families during the state of calamity. Likewise, we recommended to instruct the Municipal Accountant to adjust the affected accounts, and for succeeding completed/purchased PPE, recognize in the general fund book upon issuance to the end user in compliance with Item 5.1.14 of COA Circular 2012-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly observe the proper recording and accounting of payment of salaries of Job Order employees in conformance with Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare a complete set of FS for each of the LGUs' economic enterprises, which shall be attached to the consolidated FS of the General Fund in order to provide adequate information on the financial position, performance, and net assets/equity of the enterprises.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer to maintain a cashbook and record cash-related transactions as required in Section 174 of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual (GAAM), Volume 1. Moreover, a separate  Cashbook must be maintained for each fund to provide recording and to facilitate immediate verification of cash accountability at any given time, as well as to enhance easy reporting and monitoring thereof.

We recommended that Management for the succeeding activities of the Special Education Fund that require Cash Advances, transfer the fund directly to the implementing agency, and the transfer be recorded in the Due from NGAs account so that the Municipality can monitor the implementation of the proposed activities effectively.
We recommended that Management ensure that succeeding advances for the AICS program will be assigned to AO at MSWDO as required by Item 3.2 of COA Circular 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

We recommended that Management instruct Heads of Departments to request PCF to review their monthly recurring petty operating expenses, ensuring a realistic PCF amount is set up that is sufficient for one month’s use.
We recommended that Management require the Petty Cash custodians to maintain cashbooks as required under Chapters II and III of the Revised Cash Examination Manual.

We recommended that Management ensure that all petty cash funds under the custody of the AOs be kept in a locked cabinet to provide reasonable assurance that these are safeguarded from possible misuse and loss due to theft.

We recommend the following to the Management:
Ensure that all obligations are valid claims supported with approved documentary requirements and sufficient evidence;
Ensure that accounting procedures for budgetary accounts and check disbursement process are observed as provided in Sections 11 and 44 of Chapter 3, Volume 1 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs; and
For future improvement opportunities, design an internal timeline for processing valid claims to avoid delay in the settlement of obligations.
In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management require the former Special Disbursing Officer to settle her account or refund the unliquidated cash advances totaling P1,866,324.99.

We recommended that Management submit justification on the payment of hazard pay and submit the required documents that should have been included in its payroll. Moreover, on the succeeding payments of hazard pay of MSWDO, it is essential to ensure compliance with Section 5 of DBM and DSWD Joint Circular No. 01 s. 2018 and Item 7.2 of DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 5, s. 2022.

We recommended that (1) the LGU Inspectorate Team refrain from accepting a project when the project work items are not yet 100% accomplished in compliance with COA Circular 2012-003; and (2) the Municipal  Accountant to review the documents attached to the disbursement vouchers to ensure the regularity of payments.

We recommended that Management instruct the Office of the General Services Officer to notify the COA Auditor of the delivery of procured goods and furnish the corresponding copies of the Acceptance and Inspection Reports (AIRs) within twenty-four (24) hours of acceptance.
We recommended that Management properly fill out the PRs and POs on the succeeding procurement of the Municipality, in accordance with the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) Manual, Volume 2.
We recommended that Management require the GSO to update PAR to monitor and control accountability over the issuance of PPE to end-users in accordance with Section 158 of COA Circular 92-386.
We recommended that Management require the contractors to rectify the above-mentioned deficiencies noted by the State Technical Audit Specialist during inspection.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Agriculture Office to fast track the conduct of the physical inventory of the LGU’s biological assets taking into consideration its biological transformation and submit a report thereon to the Municipal Accountant to update its records and correct its balance in the books of accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the OIC-Municipal Treasurer comply with the rules set forth in COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.  Cash advances for the current year shall not be used to pay expenses of the previous years or on reimbursement basis. Likewise, the Municipality is highly encouraged to pay expenses directly to suppliers, officers and employees through checks. But on instances when it may be very difficult, impractical or impossible to make payments by check, payments shall be made under the petty cash. Hence, the LGU should stop using the cash advances for operating expenses account and should properly establish petty cash for each and every department in accordance with the rules on granting and utilization of regular cash advances for petty operating expenses under the same Circular.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to enforce collection and settlement of the long outstanding receivables and cash advances through issuance of demand letters.

In view of the foregoing cash shortage, we recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer the immediate restitution of missing funds without prejudice to the filing of appropriate charges against her for violation of Section 40 of Republic Act No. 10951, amending Article 217 of the Revised Penal Code, for malversation of public funds or property. She should also be immediately relieved from her functions involving the possession of Municipal funds.

We highly recommended the following:
The Municipal Treasury shall submit the Report of Collections and Deposits of the noted difference in the Statement of Accountability for Accountable Forms to be accounted properly.
The Local Chief Executive shall require the involved accountable officers to submit a written explanation for the delayed remittance of collections totaling P619,852.40 and assess if disciplinary action is needed. Likewise, the Municipal Treasurer shall require all accountable officers to religiously remit their collections on daily basis.
The Local Chief Executive shall require the Municipal Treasurer to immediately produce, submit and remit all accountable forms not yet reported and remitted for proper accounting and reporting. Otherwise, appropriate actions may be imposed on the Municipal Treasurer and concerned accountable officers for failure to render accounts.
Lastly, we strictly recommended that the Municipal Treasurer to exercise the diligence of a good father of a family in supervising accountable officers under her control as well as proper monitoring of all accountable forms to prevent the incurrence of loss of government funds and property in pursuance of Sections 69, 104 and 107 of P.D. 1445 and Section 30 of the Manual on New Government Accounting System.

We recommended that Management, through the Office of the Municipal Treasurer to intensify more the tax collections thru the formulation of strategies to improve collection of real property taxes and encourage the involvement of barangay officials in the tax campaign.

Hence, for efficiency in inventory, we recommended to the Municipal Treasurer to reflect the inventoried obsolete checks in her RAAF for immediate destruction to be witnessed by COA Auditor and representative from her Office.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following to the Management:
To require the Municipal Engineer to submit the basis of computation of the ABC and written justification for non-compliance with the DPWH Department Order No. 197 series of 2016 dated October 7, 2016; and
To strictly comply with the provisions of the afore-cited regulations in the preparation of the ABC for all succeeding projects.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following to the Management:
Instruct the concerned officials to furnish the Audit Team with copies of contracts and its supporting documents especially infrastructure projects, within five (5) days after its perfection pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001; and
Require the Municipality Accountant, Engineer and Bids and Award Committee to submit to the Audit Team the original lacking documents, or duly certified copies thereof.

We recommended that the BAC should require the winning bidders to submit the appropriate format of certification by the Insurance Commission authorizing the surety or insurance company to issue such performance security, which is specific to every awarded project as required by Section 39.2 of the revised IRR of  RA No. 9184.

We recommended that the BAC strictly comply with the procedures on receipts, opening and preliminary examination of bids as provided for in the Procurement Manual for LGUs and the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.

We recommended that Management see to it that future winning bidders comply with all the requirements set forth under the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 such as the posting of Performance Bond before the awarding of contract and contract signing.
We recommended that the BAC should see to it that the Municipality adheres strictly to the pertinent provisions of the procurement law on the issuance of Notice of Award and Notice to Proceed.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, jointly with the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and the Municipal Engineer ensure that the development projects to be funded out of the 20% Development Fund are planned and evaluated to be considered as priority in as much as there is limited sources of fund.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following to the Management:
Require the Municipal Development Council to specify the details of the identified priority projects to be charged against the 20% Development Fund to avoid generic and lump-sum projects; and
Stop the practice of implementing generic or lump-sum projects without further authority from the Sanggunian to avoid future audit suspension/disallowance.

In view thereof, we recommended that the Management require the contractors of the aforementioned projects to immediately undertake the necessary correction/rectification of the deficiencies indicated in the COA-TAS report. Thereafter, submit to us the SWA on the corrections/rectifications made together with the pictures taken during and after for re-inspection.

We recommended that Management utilize solely the LDRRMF to projects and activities under the four (4) thematic areas, namely, disaster prevention and mitigation, disaster preparedness, disaster response and disaster rehabilitation and recovery as provided in Section 5.0 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.

We recommended that Management strictly follow the computation of employer share as provided under Rule VI, Section I of the IRR of R.A. 9679 or the Home Development Mutual Fund Law of 2009, and stop the payment of excessive contributions.

We recommended that Management:
Submit written justification/explanation why the above ECRA was granted to officials, regular employees, contractual employees and employees of NGAs assigned in the Municipality;
Submit a copy of a prior approval by the Office of the President authorizing ECRA in conformity to applicable laws, rules and regulations; and
In the future, avoid granting benefits or assistance which were not among those benefits or assistance authorized under the Standardization Law as prescribed under Section 12 of RA 6758 and Section 2 of Administrative Order No. 37 dated November 21, 1998 so that the Municipality funds are spent in accordance with laws, rules and regulations.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following to the Management:
To stop the practice of procurement through reimbursement and strictly comply with the procedures under RA 9184; and
To require the concerned Municipality Officials to study and recommend measures to minimize disbursements paid through reimbursement basis.

We recommended that Management after the election ban, prioritize and consider filling-out the vacant funded positions by hiring new employees or expedite promotion of deserving employees to enhance its services to attain full implementation of programs, plans and activities of the local government unit for the optimal benefit of the constituents as well as to provide equal job opportunities to the community. Further, personnel movement has to be effected in order to acknowledge the exemplary performance of employees by way of promotion to higher rank thereby allowing the entry of new applicants for lower level positions.

We recommended the following:
The Municipal Treasurer :
Provide the list of disbursements pertaining to the outstanding balance as of September 2018 totaling P15,705,185.68.
Provide copies of withdrawals under Advances for Operating Expenses totaling P20,003,836.15 and submit the original liquidation reports totaling P29,740,167.46 for evaluation.
The Officials and Employees concerned  settle immediately their cash advances .  Further, the Management  exert efforts to demand settlement from the personnel who were no longer connected with the LGU.
The Management  establish controls particularly in safeguarding the financial resources of the agency.  Ensure that the fundamental principles are observed in processing financial transactions.

We recommended that Management through  the Municipal Accountant  make the necessary adjustments to recognize the unrecorded asset and income account.  Also, strictly adhere to the application of accrual basis of accounting and matching principle to fairly reflect the results of operations for a particular period.  The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer coordinate regularly  for the determination and monitoring of accounts receivables.

We recommended that the Management through  the Municipal Treasurer  prepare the complete and accurate list of taxpayers indicating the amount due, including delinquencies and collectible for the year.  This report shall be furnished to the Municipal Accountant which shall serve as the basis in setting up the RPT/SET Receivables for the year.

We recommended the following:
The Municipal Treasurer  ensure that payment of taxpayer must apply first to his previous unpaid taxes including its interests, penalties and surcharges before settling their taxes for the current year.
Certified list of outstanding RPT and SET due and demandable from taxpayers  be prepared at the beginning of the year.
In cases wherein there is a failure to pay the basic real property tax or any other tax levied when due, the Municipal Treasurer shall subject the taxpayer to the payment of interest at the rate of two percent (2%) per month on the unpaid amount or a fraction thereof, until the delinquent tax shall have been fully paid. Provided, however, that in no case shall the total interest on the unpaid tax or portion thereof exceed thirty-six (36) months.

We recommended that Management through  the Municipal Assessor  complete the General Revision of Assessment and Property Classification for 2018 at the earliest possible time and undertake revisions thereon every three (3) years thereafter.
